tracks.name: Theravāda
tracks.subtitle: Path of the Elders
tracks.blurb: The oldest surviving Buddhist tradition. Pāli canon. Forest monasteries. Mettā and the breath. The longest path, walked one step at a time.

stages.title: Where We Begin
stages.subtitle: Before the texts, the story
stages.blurb: Sit with the master. Hear the story of how this began — a prince who saw what most never let themselves see, and what he found when he looked.

items.title: The Four Sights
items.subtitle: Buddhacarita III · Aśvaghoṣa, trans. E.B. Cowell 1894
items.blurb: Old age, sickness, death, and the renunciant. The four encounters that turned a prince into a wanderer.

items.title: The Buddha's Awakening
items.subtitle: Buddhacarita XIV · trans. E.B. Cowell 1894
items.blurb: Under the Bodhi tree at Bodh Gayā. Māra defeated; the chain of dependent origination seen end to end.

items.title: The First Sermon at Sarnath
items.subtitle: SN 56.11 · Setting the Wheel in Motion
items.blurb: Five ascetics in the deer park. The first words after silence.

stages.title: The Dhammapada
stages.subtitle: Verses to live by
stages.blurb: The most-read book in the Pāli canon. Twenty-six chapters of paired verses, each a stone you can carry.

items.title: Dhammapada
items.subtitle: All 26 chapters · 45 min total
items.blurb: The whole path in verse. Begin with the Pairs and let them turn in you for a season.

stages.title: Khuddaka Highlights
stages.subtitle: The shorter, older voice
stages.blurb: Among the oldest layers of the canon. Plain verse, blunt instruction, no commentary needed.

items.title: The Serpent · SNP 1.1
items.subtitle: SNP 1.1
items.blurb: Like a serpent shedding its old skin — that is what this practice asks.

items.title: With Cunda · SNP 1.5
items.subtitle: SNP 1.5
items.blurb: Four kinds of contemplative — and how to know which one you sit with.

items.title: Mettā Sutta · The Discourse on Love · SNP 1.8
items.subtitle: SNP 1.8
items.blurb: What loving-kindness is, in the simplest words anyone has yet found for it.

items.title: Sensual Pleasures · SNP 4.1
items.subtitle: SNP 4.1
items.blurb: What is it about wanting that keeps the wheel turning.

items.title: Awakening · Udāna 1.1
items.subtitle: UD 1.1
items.blurb: The first uplifting utterance after enlightenment.

items.title: Bāhiya · Udāna 1.10
items.subtitle: UD 1.10
items.blurb: In the seen, only the seen. Bāhiya's instant awakening.

items.title: The Heart Released · Itivuttaka 26
items.subtitle: ITI 26
items.blurb: On the unconditioned.

items.title: Vimalā · Therīgāthā 5.10
items.subtitle: THIG 5.10
items.blurb: An elder nun's verse — the first women's voices in the canon.

items.title: Belaṭṭhasīsa · Theragāthā 1.16
items.subtitle: THAG 1.16
items.blurb: An elder monk's verse.

items.title: Khuddakapāṭha 1 · The Refuges and Precepts
items.subtitle: KP 1
items.blurb: The lay person's daily commitment, distilled.

stages.title: Majjhima Nikāya
stages.subtitle: The middle-length discourses · the heart of the canon
stages.blurb: The single most rewarding Nikāya. The master's curated entry sequence — sit with each one before moving on.

items.title: Middle-Length Discourses · Signatures
items.subtitle: 12 curated suttas (152 total in canon)
items.blurb: Twelve gates into the Buddha's teaching, ordered by the master.

chapters.title: MN 22 · The Simile of the Cobra · Alagaddūpama
MN 36 · Mahāsaccaka · The Longer Discourse with Saccaka
MN 72 · Aggivacchagotta · The Fire and the Wanderer
MN 121 · Cūḷasuññata · Shorter Discourse on Emptiness
MN 140 · Dhātuvibhaṅga · The Analysis of Elements
MN 152 · Indriyabhāvanā · Development of the Faculties

stages.title: Dīgha and Saṃyutta
stages.subtitle: The long discourses · the connected ones
stages.blurb: Where Majjhima is medium-length, Dīgha goes long. Saṃyutta groups by theme.

items.title: Long Discourses · Signatures
items.subtitle: 5 curated suttas
items.blurb: Five long discourses every student should hear once.

chapters.title: DN 2 · Sāmaññaphala · The Fruits of the Contemplative Life
DN 22 · Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna · The Long Mindfulness Discourse

items.title: Connected Discourses · Signatures
items.subtitle: 6 curated samyuttas
items.blurb: Saṃyutta groups by theme. These six are the keys.

chapters.title: Anattalakkhaṇa · The Characteristic of Not-Self · SN 22.59

stages.title: Aṅguttara Nikāya
stages.subtitle: Numbered teachings
stages.blurb: Discourses arranged by the number of items they enumerate. Memory's natural shape.

items.title: Numerical Discourses · Signatures
items.subtitle: 6 curated sets across nipātas
items.blurb: When the master wants you to remember something, he counts it.

stages.title: Commentarial
stages.subtitle: After the canon, the systematizers
stages.blurb: Where the canon is dialogue, the commentaries are diagram. The Visuddhimagga is the paradigmatic text.

items.title: Visuddhimagga · The Path of Purity
items.subtitle: Buddhaghosa, 5th c. · trans. Pe Maung Tin (1922 PD)
items.blurb: The Theravāda systematic summa. Sīla, samādhi, paññā in unbroken order. Part I — Of Virtue.

stages.title: Abhidhamma
stages.subtitle: The deepest analysis
stages.blurb: The third basket. Not for everyone, not at the start. When you arrive here, you'll know.

items.title: Abhidhammattha Saṅgaha
items.subtitle: Anuruddha · the standard modern entry
items.blurb: The classical introduction to Abhidhamma — what mind is, broken down to its constituents.

tracks.name: Mahāyāna
tracks.subtitle: The Great Vehicle
tracks.blurb: Bodhicitta. The bodhisattva turn. The open canon. Sūtras that arrived centuries later, claiming the deepest authority.

stages.title: The Prajñāpāramitā Core
stages.subtitle: Wisdom-gone-beyond
stages.blurb: Two short sūtras that changed everything that came after. Form is emptiness; emptiness is form.

items.title: Heart Sūtra
items.subtitle: Prajñāpāramitā Hṛdaya · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Two hundred and sixty characters that hold the whole. Form is emptiness, emptiness is form.

items.title: Diamond Sūtra
items.subtitle: Vajracchedikā · trans. Max Müller (Sacred Books of the East 1894 PD)
items.blurb: Where the Heart is the punchline, the Diamond is the argument. Each section dissolves a place to stand.

stages.title: The Major Narrative Sūtras
stages.subtitle: Lotus, Pure Land, Vimalakīrti
stages.blurb: The Mahāyāna's most-read scriptures. Parables and visions; the Buddha-lands and the layman who out-taught the bodhisattvas.

items.title: Lotus Sūtra · Introduction
items.subtitle: Saddharmapuṇḍarīka · Ch. I · trans. Hendrik Kern (1884 PD)
items.blurb: The Buddha at Mount Gṛdhrakūṭa. Light streams from his brow. The opening of the Lotus.

items.title: Lotus Sūtra · Skilful Means
items.subtitle: Saddharmapuṇḍarīka · Ch. II · trans. Hendrik Kern (1884 PD)
items.blurb: One vehicle, taught as three. The doctrine of upāya — skilful means. The hinge of the whole Lotus.

items.title: Lotus Sūtra · The Burning House
items.subtitle: Saddharmapuṇḍarīka · Ch. III · trans. Hendrik Kern (1884 PD)
items.blurb: A father lures his children from a burning house with promises of carts. The most famous parable in Mahāyāna literature.

items.title: Larger Sukhāvatī-vyūha
items.subtitle: The Land of Bliss · trans. Max Müller (1894 PD)
items.blurb: Forty-eight vows and the founding of a Buddha-land.

items.title: Smaller Sukhāvatī-vyūha
items.subtitle: The Amida Sutra · trans. Max Müller (1894 PD)
items.blurb: The Pure Land described to Śāriputra. The short Pure Land sūtra, chanted daily across East Asia.

items.title: Amitāyur-dhyāna-sūtra
items.subtitle: Sūtra on the Meditation on Amitāyus · trans. Junjirō Takakusu (1894 PD)
items.blurb: Sixteen contemplations. The third Pure Land sūtra — a step-by-step visualization.

items.title: Vimalakīrti's Silence
items.subtitle: Vimalakīrti-nirdeśa · canonical excerpts (4 chapters)
items.blurb: The layman who out-taught the bodhisattvas. When asked the meaning of non-duality, Vimalakīrti says nothing.

stages.title: Mind-Only · The Lankāvatāra Sūtra
stages.subtitle: The sūtra Bodhidharma carried east
stages.blurb: The Yogācāra heart. Mahāmati's questions to the Buddha on Mount Lanka. Mind-only, the eight consciousnesses, self-realisation. Sit with one chapter at a time.

items.title: Lankāvatāra Sūtra
items.subtitle: Self-Realisation of Noble Wisdom · trans. D.T. Suzuki, in Goddard 1932 (PD)
items.blurb: Thirteen chapters. The text Bodhidharma handed to Huike. Mind-only, the eight consciousnesses, the path to self-realisation.

chapters.title: Right Knowledge or Knowledge of Relations
Perfect Knowledge, or Knowledge of Reality

stages.title: Avataṃsaka and the Forty-Two Sections
stages.subtitle: Cosmic vision and primer
stages.blurb: Where the Lankāvatāra is dense, the Avataṃsaka is vast. The Forty-Two Sections sūtra is the primer that came west.

items.title: Avataṃsaka · Flower Ornament Sūtra
items.subtitle: Three signature passages: Entering the Dharma Realm, the Net of Indra, the Vows of Samantabhadra
items.blurb: The most cosmic of Mahāyāna sūtras. Every grain of sand contains every Buddha-land.

items.title: Sūtra of Forty-Two Sections
items.subtitle: 5 signature sections
items.blurb: The first sūtra translated from Sanskrit into Chinese. Short pieces of advice, spoken by the Buddha.

stages.title: Synthesis · Awakening of Faith
stages.subtitle: The bridge between sūtra and śāstra
stages.blurb: The text that unified Yogācāra and tathāgatagarbha into the East Asian Mahāyāna synthesis.

items.title: The Awakening of Faith in the Mahāyāna
items.subtitle: Aśvaghoṣa (attrib.) · canonical excerpts
items.blurb: The systematic synthesis. One Mind, Suchness, the path.

chapters.title: The One Mind and Its Two Aspects

stages.title: Synthesis and the Bodhisattva Path
stages.subtitle: Śāntideva's Way
stages.blurb: The most-recommended Mahāyāna practice text in the world. Nine chapters. Begin with the Praise.

items.title: Bodhicaryāvatāra · The Way of the Bodhisattva
items.subtitle: Śāntideva (8th c.) · trans. L.D. Barnett, The Path of Light (1909 PD)
items.blurb: Nine chapters. The bodhisattva's training, from generating bodhicitta through to the perfect knowledge of emptiness.

chapters.title: The Praise of the Thought of Enlightenment
Heedfulness in the Thought of Enlightenment

stages.title: Schools of East Asia
stages.subtitle: Pure Land, Tendai, Huayan, Nichiren, Shingon
stages.blurb: Where Indian Mahāyāna becomes Japanese and Chinese sectarian Buddhism. Modern translations only.

items.title: Tannishō
items.subtitle: Yuien-bo, 13th c. · Shin Buddhism
items.blurb: The entry point to Shinran's teaching. Other-power, the nembutsu.

items.title: Treatise on the Golden Lion
items.subtitle: Fazang (643–712) · Huayan
items.blurb: Every grain a Buddha-land. The classic Huayan analogy.

tracks.name: Vajrayāna
tracks.subtitle: The Diamond Vehicle
tracks.blurb: The Tibetan path. Mantra, deity, sky. Begin with the preliminary practices — Tokmé's thirty-seven, the slogans, the songs of Milarepa.

stages.title: Entering the Vajrayāna
stages.subtitle: Tibet's lotus-born teacher
stages.blurb: Before the elaborate practices, the simplest pointing. Padmasambhava's direct words.

items.title: Padmasambhava · Direct Pointing
items.subtitle: Four short instructions
items.blurb: The lotus-born. Tibet's second Buddha. Begin with these.

stages.title: Preliminary Path
stages.subtitle: The Thirty-Seven Practices of a Bodhisattva
stages.blurb: Tokmé Zangpo's verses. Each one a stone in the foundation.

items.title: Thirty-Seven Practices of a Bodhisattva
items.subtitle: Tokmé Zangpo, 14th c. · 20 verses
items.blurb: The complete map of the bodhisattva's daily life. One verse for each direction the heart can lean.

stages.title: Lamrim and Lojong
stages.subtitle: The graduated path · the slogans
stages.blurb: Tsongkhapa's three principal aspects, then Atiśa's mind-training slogans. The Tibetan path made portable.

items.title: Three Principal Aspects of the Path
items.subtitle: Tsongkhapa (1357–1419) · the foundation of Geluk lamrim
items.blurb: Renunciation, bodhicitta, the right view of emptiness. The whole path on a single page.

items.title: Lojong · Seven Points of Mind Training
items.subtitle: Atiśa's slogans · 14 curated verses from the 59
items.blurb: The mind-training slogans that turn every circumstance into the path.

chapters.title: Rest in the Nature of Ālaya
Turn All Mishaps into the Path
Four Practices Are the Best Method
Always Maintain Only a Joyful Mind
All Activity Should Be Done with One Intention
The Two Activities — At Beginning and End

stages.title: Songs of Realization
stages.subtitle: Milarepa's voice
stages.blurb: Tibet's most-loved poet-yogi. After Marpa, after the years of practice in caves — the songs.

items.title: Songs of Milarepa
items.subtitle: Five signature songs from the Hundred Thousand Songs
items.blurb: On solitary dwelling, on food and clothing, on the hunters, on death, on the kindness of the teacher.

chapters.title: On the Joy of Solitary Dwelling
Song on the Approach of Death
On the Kindness of the Teacher

stages.title: Dzogchen and the Bardos
stages.subtitle: The path through dying and beyond
stages.blurb: Padmasambhava's terma cycle on the after-death state. Read aloud to the dying — and listened to as practice while still alive.

items.title: Bardo Thödol · The Tibetan Book of the Dead
items.subtitle: trans. Lāma Kazi Dawa-Samdup, ed. W.Y. Evans-Wentz (Oxford 1927 PD)
items.blurb: Three transitional states. The Clear Light, the wrathful and peaceful deities, the closing of the wombs.

chapters.title: Chikhai Bardo · The Moments of Death
Chönyid Bardo · The Experiencing of Reality

stages.title: Pith Material
stages.subtitle: The marrow
stages.blurb: Where the lineage gathers itself into a few sentences.

items.title: Patrul Rinpoche · Pith Advice
items.subtitle: From Words of My Perfect Teacher · canonical excerpt
items.blurb: The Nyingma master's directness.

items.title: Three Kinds of Practitioner
items.subtitle: From the Lamp for the Path · canonical excerpt
items.blurb: Lesser, middling, greatest. Atiśa's threefold typology.

tracks.name: Zen / Chan / Seon
tracks.subtitle: A special transmission outside the scriptures
tracks.blurb: Zen says the truth doesn't depend on words — and then leaves us a thousand books. Sit first. The texts come second.

stages.title: Sit Down First
stages.subtitle: Before any text, the silence
stages.blurb: Hear the story of how this came to China, and from China to the rest of the world.

items.title: Huineng's Verse on the Wall
items.subtitle: Platform Sūtra · Ch. I · trans. Wong Mou-lam (1930), in Goddard 1932 PD
items.blurb: An illiterate woodcutter hears the Diamond Sūtra in a marketplace, walks thirty days to Wong-mui, and writes the verse that wins the Patriarchate.

stages.title: The Sūtras Chan Inherits
stages.subtitle: Mahāyāna texts the Zen masters wouldn't admit they read
stages.blurb: Zen says 'a special transmission outside the scriptures' — and yet every Zen master reads these. Sit with each one before turning a page.

items.title: Heart Sūtra
items.subtitle: Prajñāpāramitā Hṛdaya · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Two hundred and sixty characters. Form is emptiness, emptiness is form.

items.title: Diamond Sūtra
items.subtitle: Vajracchedikā · trans. Max Müller (1894 PD)
items.blurb: Where the Heart is the punchline, the Diamond is the argument.

stages.title: Early Chan
stages.subtitle: 5th–7th century · before the great masters
stages.blurb: The seed-texts. Short, blunt, anti-systematic. Read each one once before any commentary.

items.title: Two Entrances and Four Practices
items.subtitle: Bodhidharma · trans. D.T. Suzuki (Manual of Zen Buddhism, 1934 PD)
items.blurb: The only text scholars give Bodhidharma with reasonable confidence. Entrance by Reason and Entrance by Conduct.

items.title: Outline of Practice
items.subtitle: Bodhidharma · 4 of the four practices
items.blurb: On hatred, on conditions, on seeking, on practising the Dharma. The four practices unpacked.

chapters.title: The Second Practice · Adapting to Conditions
The Fourth Practice · Practicing the Dharma

items.title: Xinxin Ming · Verses on Faith-Mind
items.subtitle: Sengcan, Third Patriarch · 5 sections
items.blurb: The Perfect Way is only difficult for those who pick and choose. The earliest Zen poem we have.

items.title: Song of Enlightenment (Shōdōka)
items.subtitle: Yongjia Xuanjue (665–713) · trans. D.T. Suzuki (Manual of Zen Buddhism, 1934 PD)
items.blurb: Yoka Daishi's hymn after one night with the Sixth Patriarch.

items.title: Platform Sūtra · Key Verses
items.subtitle: Huineng · 4 signature passages
items.blurb: The Sixth Patriarch's verse, his teaching on self-nature, on seeing one's nature, on no-thought.

stages.title: Tang Masters
stages.subtitle: 8th–9th century · the golden age
stages.blurb: The masters Zen takes itself from. Encounter dialogues, not systematic doctrine. Sit and let them strike.

items.title: Mazu and Shitou (Sekito) · Two Great Masters of the T'ang
items.subtitle: trans. D.T. Suzuki (Manual of Zen Buddhism, 1934 PD)
items.blurb: Biography and dialogues. Mazu the Father, Shitou the Stone-head. The lineage that all Zen comes from.

items.title: Huangbo · Treatise on the Transmission of Mind
items.subtitle: Huangbo Xiyun (d. 850) · trans. D.T. Suzuki (1934 PD)
items.blurb: By the Dharma is meant Mind. Huangbo's sermon to Pei-hsiu, the prefect.

items.title: Sayings of Linji
items.subtitle: Linji Yixuan (d. 866) · 3 key sayings
items.blurb: If you meet the Buddha, kill the Buddha. The true person of no rank. Nothing lacking.

chapters.title: The True Person of No Rank

stages.title: Song Dynasty Koan Collections
stages.subtitle: The curated digest of Tang-era encounter dialogues
stages.blurb: When the masters had been gone too long to ask in person, their students gathered the cases.

items.title: Mumonkan · The Gateless Gate
items.subtitle: Wumen Huikai, 1228 · 25 of 48 cases
items.blurb: Forty-eight cases. Each one a gate. The gateless gate.

chapters.title: The Buddha Holds Up a Flower
Not the Wind, Not the Flag
The Cypress Tree in the Garden
Step Forward from the Top of the Pole

items.title: Blue Cliff Record · Selected Cases
items.subtitle: Yuanwu Keqin, 1125 · 5 of 100 cases
items.blurb: Built on Xuedou's verses. Yuanwu's commentary. The Song-dynasty digest of Tang encounter dialogues.

chapters.title: Ummon's Every Day Is a Good Day

items.title: Hakuin · Song of Zazen
items.subtitle: Hakuin Ekaku (1686–1769) · 3 verses
items.blurb: The formative Rinzai reformer's anthem. All beings are originally Buddha.

stages.title: Dōgen and Sōtō
stages.subtitle: 13th century · Genjokoan
stages.blurb: Dōgen is the one Japanese figure who functions as a primary source on the level of the Tang Chinese masters. Read his own words.

items.title: Shōbōgenzō · Genjokoan
items.subtitle: Eihei Dōgen (1200–1253) · 4 signature passages
items.blurb: To study the Way is to study the self. Firewood does not become ash. Water and the moon. The bird's path.

stages.title: Later Japanese Masters
stages.subtitle: 14th–18th century
stages.blurb: After Dōgen, the tradition deepens. Bankei, Bassui, Hakuin shape modern practice.

items.title: Bankei · The Unborn
items.subtitle: Bankei Yōtaku (1622–1693) · Waddell trans. ©
items.blurb: The great anti-systematic teacher. Stay with the Unborn.

items.title: Bassui · Mud and Water
items.subtitle: Bassui Tokushō (1327–1387) · Braverman trans. ©
items.blurb: Who is the master of all this?

stages.title: Korean Seon
stages.subtitle: 12th century
stages.blurb: Korean Seon's foundational systematizer.

items.title: Tracing Back the Radiance
items.subtitle: Jinul / Chinul (1158–1210) · Buswell 1991 ©
items.blurb: The Korean equivalent of Dōgen for systematic depth.

tracks.name: Secular
tracks.subtitle: Practice without lineage
tracks.blurb: The teachings as a way of attention. No metaphysics required.

stages.title: Practice Notes
stages.subtitle: The bare instructions
stages.blurb: Short notes — what to do, where to put attention. No tradition required.

items.title: Working with Anger
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Anger

items.title: Working with Anxiety
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Anxiety

items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Body Scan

items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Breath

items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Eating

items.title: On What Is Enough
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Reflections · Enough

items.title: End of the Day
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Evening Reflection

items.title: Sitting with Grief
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Grief

items.title: Reflection on Change
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Reflections · Impermanence

items.title: Listening as Practice
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Listening

items.title: Loving-Kindness
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Loving-Kindness

items.title: Setting an Intention
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Morning Intention

items.title: Outdoor Meditation
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Outdoors

items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Devices

items.title: Falling Asleep
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Sleep

items.title: Walking Meditation
items.subtitle: Practice notes · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Practice Notes · Walking

stages.title: Verses
stages.subtitle: Lines to carry
stages.blurb: A handful of verses for everyday practice.

items.title: On Comparing
items.subtitle: Verses for practice · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Verses · Comparison

items.title: Kindness as Default
items.subtitle: Verses for practice · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Verses · Kindness First

items.title: Only This Moment
items.subtitle: Verses for practice · existing canonical entry
items.blurb: Verses · The Present
